items.title: Trial date set for man accused of drug possession with intent to supply in Burnley
items.content_text: A Burnley man accused of possessing drugs with intent to supply will stand trial later this year after pleading not guilty.

items.title: Woman was supplying drugs to neighbours to fund her own habit
items.content_text: A Nelson woman who admitted supplying cannabis to neighbours to fund her own drug habit has been given a community order.

items.title: ‘A moment we feared would never come’ - families celebrate lifeline for care homes
items.content_text: The niece of a woman who lives in one of the Lancashire County Council-run care homes whose futures had been in doubt says confirmation that none of them will be closed marks a “victory” in the community campaigns to protect the facilities.

items.title: Residents baffled by bus station shake-up — but councillors say fears are unfounded
items.content_text: Colne residents and businesses are confused about a bus station revamp and traffic flow changes – but local councillors insist it will improve things for everyone.

items.title: New Clitheroe pump track planned as £100,000 funding set aside
items.content_text: Clitheroe could get a new pump track after Ribble Valley Borough Council earmarked up to £100,000 from the UK Shared Prosperity Fund for the project

items.title: Burnley residents urged to reject Blackburn merger in devolution consultation
items.content_text: The leader of Burnley Council has urged borough residents to reject a merger with Blackburn in a local government reorganisation consultation.

items.title: Burnley man jailed after threatening to kill child after sexually assaulting her
items.content_text: A Burnley man who threatened to kill a teenager if she did not let him sexually assault her has been jailed

items.title: In response to my post about the launch of the 'National Cancer Plan' on substack, someone responsed with:  "Great. But...
items.content_text:
In response to my post about the launch of the 'National Cancer Plan' on substack, someone responsed with:

"Great. But how about ending homelessness, taxing the rich  nationalising water and ending the cost of living crisis?"

So I responsed with this...

1/9

items.title: Future Olympics could be held in the North of England as leaders say 'we are ready'
items.content_text: The North of England could host the Olympics for the first time, as mayors and leaders propose a UK bid anchored in the region, aiming to accelerate regeneration and unity.
